Home › Services › GST Registration

GST Registration in Mumbai

GST registration is the process of obtaining a GSTIN so a business can legally collect GST and claim input tax credit. We handle the entire application, from documents to GSTIN, for businesses in Mumbai.

Registration is mandatory once aggregate turnover exceeds ₹40 lakh for goods or ₹20 lakh for services in most states, and in certain cases (inter-state supply, e-commerce, casual taxable persons) regardless of turnover — as per the CGST Act, 2017. Voluntary registration is also permitted.

What is the process?

  1. Assess whether registration is required and the correct registration type.
  2. Collect and verify documents (PAN, proof of business, address proof, bank details).
  3. File the application on the GST portal and respond to any clarification.
  4. Receive the GSTIN and registration certificate.

What documents are required?

  • PAN of the business/proprietor
  • Aadhaar of the proprietor/partners/directors
  • Proof of business address (electricity bill / rent agreement / NOC)
  • Bank account proof (cancelled cheque or statement)
  • Photographs and constitution documents
Typical timelineTypically 7–14 working days after complete documents, subject to GST department processing and any Aadhaar authentication.
FeesFees depend on entity type and complexity. Contact us for a quote.

Source: CGST Act, 2017 and CBIC notifications — gst.gov.in. Thresholds vary for special-category states; we confirm the rule for your case.

Request a quote

Frequently asked questions

When is GST registration mandatory?
GST registration is mandatory once aggregate turnover exceeds ₹40 lakh for goods or ₹20 lakh for services in most states, and in specific cases such as inter-state supply or e-commerce regardless of turnover, as per the CGST Act, 2017.
How long does GST registration take?
It usually takes about 7 to 14 working days after all documents are in order, depending on GST department processing and Aadhaar authentication.
Can I register voluntarily below the threshold?
Yes. Voluntary GST registration is allowed and can help a business claim input tax credit and supply to GST-registered customers.
What is the official GST portal?
The official portal is the Goods and Services Tax Network at gst.gov.in, operated under CBIC.
